name: display sentinel: 'what you don't clock until it's too late' is allowed (PC-POV delayed realization)

source.purpose: Playthrough 10 produced legitimate in-character delayed-realization prose after a failed roll: 'What you don't clock until it's too late is that you stopped in the doorway.' This is PC-POV retrospective insight — the failed roll's cost surfaces from the PC's own perspective, not as omniscient narrator sidebar. Without suppression, the narrator_reveal scanner would fire on 'what you don't' and false-positive on legitimate prose. Added a realization-tail lookahead suppression.

narrator.prose: What you don't clock until it's too late is that you stopped in the doorway. Two full seconds. Long enough that Calder's head comes up from his terminal.
